action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py failed in eval with a broadcasting error in nerf_renderer.py, but test_predict.py succeeded locally on 1 object. Analysis: The error is likely an OOM (Out Of Memory) that occurs after processing a few objects due to memory leaks, which manifests as a failed tensor allocation during the rendering step (line 116). I will fix the `torch.cross` deprecation warning, add explicit memory management (`gc.collect()` and `torch.cuda.empty_cache()`) after each object, and submit an eval. I will also run a script to save the source code of `NeRFRenderer.forward` to a file, so I can inspect the exact chunking logic if it fails again.
